**Step 2 (StepbyStep Logic):** S88. Ans.(b) 
Sol. Ram Navami will be celebrated on an even date in the month having 30 days, so it will be celebrated on 2nd either 
in April or in June. Thus, two possibilities arise from this statement. Holi will be celebrated two months before Ram 
Navami. 

Buddha Purnima will be celebrated on the 5th and just after the month in which Eid will be celebrated. Diwali will be 
celebrated in March but not just before the month of Eid. The sum of the dates of the two festivals which will be 
celebrated in a single month is 25, which means the festivals on the 2nd and 23rd will be celebrated in a single month. 

Lohri will be celebrated on the 13th but not in May. Here, case 2 is ruled out. Holi will be on the 29th as the year is a 
leap year. 

Eid will not be celebrated on the 7th, so it will be celebrated on the 17th. Also. Diwali will be celebrated on the 7th and 
Vaisakhi will be celebrated on the 23rd in April. Hence, the final arrangement is: 

Eid will be celebrated in May.
